items.title: Is AI Hollywood's New Cosmetic Surgery?
items.excerpt: Studios and producers are quietly using AI to polish films in post-production while keeping it off the credits, with one leading AI technologist noting his company is credited on only one-fifth of the projects it completes. The piece examines why Hollywood fears audience backlash even when using AI for imperceptible enhancements rather than generative content.

items.title: The AI Issue 2026
items.excerpt: Hollywood Reporter's special AI issue examines who the AI guides in entertainment are and where they are taking the industry, covering filmmaking workflows, production pipelines, and the creative AI tools reshaping how films are made. The package features voices from across the studio ecosystem on the present and future of AI in production.

items.title: Annecy's MIFA Bets on Cross-IP, AI and Global Growth Amid Animation Financing Squeeze
items.excerpt: Annecy's co-production market MIFA is doubling down on AI-focused workshops and cross-IP deal-making in 2026 as animation financing tightens globally. The report covers how AI tools are increasingly central to pitches and co-development strategies at the world's leading animation industry market, with a philosophy of 'artist-first, AI second'.

items.title: Adobe Announces Acquisition of Topaz Labs, an AI Video and Image Enhancement Company
items.excerpt: Adobe is acquiring Topaz Labs — makers of Gigapixel AI, Video AI, and DeNoise — to fold its best-in-class AI enhancement tools into the Firefly ecosystem and Creative Cloud applications. The deal signals Adobe's intent to own the full stack of AI-powered restoration, upscaling, and noise-removal for professional filmmakers and editors.

items.title: Adobe Announces Massive Expansion of Its Agentic AI Capabilities in Firefly and Apps like Premiere
items.excerpt: Adobe is expanding creative agents across Firefly and Creative Cloud apps including Premiere Pro, enabling agentic AI workflows that can autonomously execute multi-step editing tasks. The update marks a shift from AI-assisted tools to AI-driven production agents embedded directly in post-production software.

items.title: Adobe Creative Cloud June 2026: everything that's new
items.excerpt: Adobe's June 2026 Creative Cloud update brings rebuilt AI roto tools to After Effects, an offline Remove Tool, AI culling for photos, and new effects and agentic capabilities across Premiere and Lightroom. The comprehensive rundown covers every AI-assisted workflow change across the full CC suite relevant to editors and VFX artists.

items.title: The Model Doesn't Matter: Inside the Race to Be the AI Production Platform Filmmakers Want to Use
items.excerpt: An in-depth analysis of the AI production platforms — Artlist Studio, ComfyUI, Flora, and Amazon's Project Nara — competing to become the standard workflow tool for professional filmmakers, with ComfyUI currently holding de-facto industry-standard status as an open-source, customizable foundation. The piece explores why platform cohesion and filmmaker experience, not raw model quality, is the real battleground for adoption.

items.title: I Saw Hell Grind, the AI-Generated Film That Premiered in Cannes. It Was Shocking and Realistic
items.excerpt: A first-person account of watching Hell Grind, an 80-minute feature made in 14 days for $500K using ByteDance Seedance and Higgsfield tools, which fooled many viewers into thinking scenes were live-action. The piece offers a production-level breakdown of how AI filmmaking tools have reached a threshold where feature-length cinematic output can rival traditional productions.

items.title: Asteria And LTX Form Strategic Partnership With Goal Of Advancing Cinematic AI Storytelling
items.excerpt: Natasha Lyonne's AI production studio Asteria has partnered with open-weights model maker LTX, using LTX's HDR IC-LoRA to integrate hand-drawn 2D, CG, and AI animation into scene-linear 16-bit HDR — as demonstrated in Tribeca-selected short ChikaBOOM!. The partnership targets a key production gap by bringing generative AI video up to professional-grade colorimetric standards.

items.title: StoReel Launches Canvas, AI Production Platform Targeting Microdrama Creators
items.excerpt: StoReel has launched Canvas, an AI-native end-to-end production platform that takes a script and auto-generates scene breakdowns, storyboards, character references, and optimized prompts for leading AI video models, cutting production time by two-thirds at roughly 15% of live-action costs. The launch follows StoReel's $34 million financing round and positions the platform for the rapidly growing microdrama creator market.

items.title: Looks Like a Seedance 2.5 Model is on the Way With 30-Second Generation Capabilities
items.excerpt: ByteDance's Seedance 2.5 model is reportedly set to launch in early July with the ability to generate video clips up to 30 seconds natively — a significant leap beyond current AI video tools that stitch shorter segments together. This would remove one of the key creative constraints limiting AI models for filmmakers building longer narrative sequences.
